checks.evidence: 4 destination/article regions and 8 visible headings detected.
6 monetary values detected; budget wording present.

6 enabled controls lacked a detectable name; 10 unique controls received focus across 12 Tab presses.
4 destination/article regions and 8 visible headings detected.
6 monetary values detected; budget wording present.
button "Vote3" changed visible text or control state.
Winner or itinerary content was visible or reachable. 2 visible itinerary regions detected.
6 enabled controls lacked a detectable name; 9 unique controls received focus across 12 Tab presses.

guardrailFacts.corePathDefinition: npm run check failed, or at least two core acceptance items failed at both viewports.

findings.title: Voting can produce seven votes for a six-person group
findings.evidence: The initial destination counts total six votes (source/src/App.tsx:10-14), while `voted` starts as null and the UI says the user's vote is still open (source/src/App.tsx:61). The first vote increments a destination without removing an existing vote, although the same panel declares “6 people” and “Everyone gets one favourite.”
findings.impact: Casting the advertised open vote produces seven votes for six people, so totals and the apparent group preference are internally inconsistent.
findings.remediation: Model the current member's existing vote explicitly or initialize the other members with at most five votes, and derive displayed participation totals from the same electorate state.

findings.title: A tie is presented as a sole front-runner
findings.evidence: Lisbon starts at three votes and Copenhagen at two; voting for Copenhagen creates a 3–3 tie. `leader` nevertheless selects the first element of a stable descending sort, while source/src/App.tsx:61 crowns only index zero and source/src/App.tsx:64 labels that destination the current front-runner.
findings.impact: The winner and itinerary can be asserted for Lisbon when the actual voting result is tied, misrepresenting the core group decision.
findings.remediation: Derive all destinations at the maximum vote count and render an explicit tied state or a documented tiebreak before selecting a winner and itinerary.

findings.title: Budget compatibility message ignores the selected limit
findings.evidence: The range permits €400, but the note unconditionally says the leader fits the budget. With the initial €460 Lisbon leader and the slider set to €400, the UI still renders “Lisbon fits your budget.”
findings.impact: Users receive false guidance in a core comparison dimension and may select a destination exceeding their stated budget.
findings.remediation: Compare `leader.budget` with `budget` and render an accurate fits/exceeds state, including the difference when over budget.

findings.title: Six controls have no accessible name
findings.evidence: The search and notification icon buttons, all three heart buttons, and the budget range input have neither visible labels nor `aria-label`/associated `<label>` elements. The supplied desktop and mobile checks independently detected exactly six enabled unnamed controls.
findings.impact: Screen-reader users cannot identify the purpose of repeated destination actions, the header controls, or the core budget slider.
findings.remediation: Give each icon button a destination-specific accessible name and associate a descriptive label with the range input.

findings.title: Core comparison text uses insufficient contrast
findings.evidence: On the white cards, `.card-meta` renders 10px travel text as `#989891` (about 2.90:1 contrast), `.itinerary-item p` uses 10px `#999a94` (about 2.84:1), and `.range-labels` uses 9px `#aaa9a3` (about 2.36:1). These are normal-sized text and fall below the 4.5:1 threshold.
findings.impact: Travel times, itinerary places, vote information, and budget bounds are repeatedly difficult to read for users with low vision or reduced contrast sensitivity.
findings.remediation: Darken these foreground colors to achieve at least 4.5:1 against their actual card backgrounds and verify all small metadata states.

findings.title: Primary navigation never opens or moves to the selected view
findings.evidence: Desktop navigation handlers only update `tab`; the main content remains unconditionally rendered at source/src/App.tsx:50-65. The duplicated mobile handlers at source/src/App.tsx:67 similarly produce only the status toast at line 68 rather than navigating to Explore, Itinerary, or Budget.
findings.impact: Prominent enabled navigation misleadingly appears to switch views while users remain on the same long overview page, though the core sections remain reachable by scrolling.
findings.remediation: Connect navigation to real conditional views or section anchors, or remove button semantics for unavailable destinations.

findings.title: Enabled destination-management controls are inert
findings.evidence: The prominent “Add destination” button and every destination heart button are rendered as enabled `<button>` elements without click handlers. The hearts only mirror the separate voting state and cannot be operated themselves.
findings.impact: Users are invited to add or favorite destinations but those secondary actions provide no response.
findings.remediation: Implement the promised actions or present them as clearly unavailable/noninteractive affordances.

findings.title: “View full itinerary” reveals no fuller itinerary
findings.evidence: The four itinerary items are already always visible. Activating “View full itinerary” only adds a one-line message that flights and stays will appear later, while changing the button label to “Hide preview.”
findings.impact: A meaningful enabled action does not deliver the additional itinerary detail its label promises.
findings.remediation: Reveal actual expanded itinerary content, or rename the control to describe the status information it toggles.

findings.title: Toast dismissal is a pointer-only SVG
findings.evidence: The navigation toast attaches `onClick` directly to the `X` SVG without a button element, keyboard handler, focusability, or accessible name.
findings.impact: Keyboard and assistive-technology users cannot focus or directly activate the visible dismissal action.
findings.remediation: Wrap the icon in a semantic button with an accessible “Dismiss” name and standard focus styling.

categories.label: Brief fidelity and functional correctness
evidence: source/src/App.tsx:10-20 supplies three destinations with budgets, travel times, ratings, tags, and a four-stop Lisbon itinerary.
source/src/App.tsx:31-34 implements interactive one-choice voting, and the supplied desktop and mobile checks confirmed a vote changed visible state.
The supplied checks passed destination visibility, budget, travel time, winner/itinerary reachability, backend-free operation, and browser-error checks at both viewports.
Several secondary enabled actions and the primary navigation do not deliver their labels' promised behavior, as detailed in the low-severity correctness findings.

categories.label: Reliability and state integrity
evidence: source/src/App.tsx:24-34 uses immutable destination updates and derives the leader from current vote state.
The electorate initialization, tie selection, and unconditional budget-fit message create three reproducible state contradictions in core vote and budget results.
The supplied interaction path reported zero console and page errors on desktop and mobile.

categories.label: Accessibility
evidence: The supplied browser checks found keyboard focus reached multiple controls, but failed accessibility at both viewports because six enabled controls lacked detectable names.
source/src/App.tsx:51-62 confirms the unnamed icon buttons and slider; source/src/App.tsx:68 also implements a pointer-only dismissal control.
source/src/index.css:2 repeatedly applies sub-4.5:1 colors to small travel, budget, vote, and itinerary text.

categories.label: Maintainability and code clarity
evidence: source/src/App.tsx:8-21 centralizes typed destination and itinerary sample data rather than duplicating card content.
source/src/App.tsx:31-34 contains the voting transition in one named function, while leader selection is a single derived value at line 29.
No separate actionable maintainability defect was found; the current state-model defects are reported under reliability rather than duplicated here.

categories.label: Security, privacy, and dependency compliance
evidence: The supplied dependency comparison passed with no added, removed, or changed packages, and source/package.json:12-24 contains only the frozen React, Lucide, Tailwind, TypeScript, and Vite dependencies.

The supplied scan found no remote references, both browser paths made no external requests, and source/src/App.tsx:10-34 uses local sample constants and component state without data collection or backend calls.
No unsafe HTML injection, secret-like values, or risky URL handling is present in the reviewed source.

categories.label: Performance and responsive robustness
evidence: The supplied production build totals 270,530 bytes; its JavaScript and CSS outputs gzip to 66.45 kB and 11.61 kB respectively.
source/src/index.css:3-4 defines tablet and mobile layouts, including a contained horizontal destination scroller and fixed mobile navigation.
The deterministic checks measured zero document-level horizontal overflow at both 1440×900 and 390×844, with no remote-asset dependency.

categories.label: Verification readiness
evidence: source/package.json:6-10 provides explicit typecheck, build, and combined check scripts.
The supplied `npm run check` completed successfully, including strict TypeScript checking and a Vite production build.

The deterministic browser paths exercised voting and the core content at both required viewports with zero uncaught errors; the state contradictions identified above remain important targets for behavior-level coverage.

strengths: The complete core comparison experience is available without sign-in, backend access, external requests, or remote media.
Destination data exposes budget, travel time, ratings, tags, vote totals, and a simple itinerary in one responsive overview.
Voting visibly updates destination counts, group bars, selection state, and the derived itinerary heading.
The project passes the supplied strict typecheck and production build, with unchanged dependencies and modest compressed assets.
Responsive rules avoid document-level overflow at both benchmark viewports.
risks: The voting model can exceed the declared electorate and incorrectly declare a winner during a tie.
Budget guidance can state that an unaffordable leader fits the selected limit.
Unnamed controls, low-contrast core metadata, and a pointer-only dismissal create repeated accessibility barriers.
Several prominent navigation and secondary-action controls behave like prototype affordances rather than completed product actions.
recommendation: Correct the electorate, tie, and budget-fit state model first; then label and restyle the inaccessible controls and metadata. Before release, connect or remove the misleading navigation, destination-management, and itinerary-expansion actions and verify those states at both benchmark viewports.
